changes.reason: Run the strategist's squeeze efficiency experiment with admission quality only. Current squeeze_long has controlled tail risk (-208.25 worst MAE) but only 187.16 PnL/exposure hour; lifting the VWAP slope floor from 0.04 to 0.05 tests whether stronger continuation improves exposure efficiency without another target-extension repeat.

Compress fixed realization slightly from 3.75 to 3.25 instead of extending targets. Latest median selected TP is very large, but average hold is 23.1m and no upside-extension candidates were found; a faster fixed scalp should improve risk-adjusted monetization if the edge is already present.

Lower the adaptive cap from 6.5 to 5.5 to prevent squeeze entries from drifting into oversized dynamic targets while preserving more upside than pullback/charm-pin profiles. This is a faster-realization test, not a broad entry filter.

expected_effect: Expected to improve squeeze_long PnL per exposure hour and average hold while keeping its low MAE profile intact. Tradeoff is possible truncation of a few convex squeeze winners; reject if squeeze PnL falls materially, completed trades drop more than 10%, total net profit falls below 98% of run_001, or VALID_0DTE performance deteriorates.
label: squeeze-fast-realization
rationale: Use exactly one legal family: batch_b_squeeze_long_take_profit. The prior accepted squeeze package expanded targets and helped from a weaker base, but the current leader already has large selected TP distances, no upside-extension candidates, and squeeze_long average hold is longer than desired. This tests a different mechanism: stronger continuation admission plus faster monetization, while avoiding global vetoes, 1DTE tightening, call-wall loosening, stale-data changes, and repeated charm-wall max-distance follow-through.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: squeeze_long produced 10 VALID_0DTE trades, $721 PnL, no slow >=12h trades, worst MAE -208.25, median MFE 77.78, but only 187.16 PnL/exposure hour and 23.1 minute average hold. The latest report has no upside TP extension candidates, so faster realization is more defensible than further target expansion.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Squeeze Fast Realization Classifier
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Lowering targets may cut profitable continuation tails and reduce total net profit even if exposure hours improve.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE squeeze_long entries with confirmed option context and ES VWAP slope strong enough for continuation.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: squeeze_long PnL/exposure hour improves, total net profit remains >=98% of run_001, completed trades remain within 10%, worst MAE stays no worse than -225 for squeeze_long, and slow_12h_trades remains 0.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long remains profitable at $1217.3 over 33 trades but owns the global -527 MAE. Static config filters overlap protected extended-VWAP winners, so the clean separator is early path failure rather than another broad threshold.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Path-aware logic can overfit a small number of delayed recoveries and block clean high-extension winners unless protected-winner retention is validated.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long high-extension or recent-high-rejection entry that fails to make early positive progress after entry.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast extended-VWAP winners, total PnL >=98% of leader, and slow trades remain 0.
